title: CV Data Model
description: Structured CV representation for the job_search optimization pipeline. Supports English (en) and Spanish (es) language variants with technical keywords preserved in English.

$schema.description: Reference to the JSON Schema version used for validation.

lang.description: Root language discriminator. 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish. Technical keywords (skills, tools, job titles, methodologies) MUST remain in English regardless of this value.

professionalSummary.description: 3-5 scannable bullets summarizing key career achievements and value proposition.

coreCompetencies.description: 4-6 competency bullets mapping to JD must-haves. Each pairs a competency area with a brief achievement-backed description.

skills.description: Categorized technical and soft skills. Each entry groups related skills under a category label.

professionalExperience.description: Primary professional experience with quantified achievements. Ordered most-recent-first.

earlierExperience.description: Simplified earlier career entries without achievement tracking. Used for roles older than the primary experience window.

contact.description: Contact information block. Name and titles are required; location and contact channels are optional.

name.description: Full name as it appears on the CV header.

titles.description: Job title(s) kept in English regardless of CV language (e.g. 'Technical Program Manager', 'Engineering Manager').

location.description: City and country, e.g. 'Buenos Aires, Argentina'.

summaryItem.description: A single professional summary bullet — action-oriented, metric-backed when possible.

text.description: The summary bullet text, e.g. 'Reduced deployment time by 40% through CI/CD pipeline automation across 3 engineering teams.'

tags.description: Forward-compatible tags for filtering and categorization.

competency.description: A core competency pairing a domain area with a brief achievement-backed justification.

title.description: Competency area label (e.g. 'Technical Program Leadership', 'Cross-Functional Collaboration').

description.description: Brief justification, ideally with a metric or concrete outcome.

skillCategory.description: A group of related skills under a category label (e.g. 'Languages & Frameworks'). All skill names MUST be in English.

category.description: Skill category label, e.g. 'Languages & Frameworks', 'Cloud & DevOps', 'Project Management'.

proficiency.description: Optional proficiency level (free-text, pending F6 structure requirements).

experience.description: A professional experience entry in the primary career window. Contains quantified achievements with structured metadata.

id.description: Stable UUID v4 identifier for cross-referencing across the pipeline. Generated via crypto.randomUUID().

role.description: Job title in English (e.g. 'Technical Program Manager').

description.description: Optional role summary paragraph (one-liner context about team scope, product, or responsibilities).

achievements.description: Quantified, impact-driven achievement bullets for this role.

achievement.description: A single quantified achievement bullet with structured metadata for programmatic matching and analysis.

id.description: Stable UUID v4 identifier. Generated via crypto.randomUUID().

text.description: The achievement bullet text (English for 'en' CV, Spanish for 'es' CV, technical keywords always in English).

metrics.description: Quantifiable metrics extracted from this achievement. Use '[NEEDS METRIC: ...]' when source lacks a verifiable number — NEVER invent values.

technologies.description: Technologies, tools, and platforms mentioned in this achievement (always in English).

domains.description: Domain classification for matching against JD requirements. Each achievement maps to one or more domains.

impact.description: Estimated business impact level of this achievement. 'high' = cross-team/customer-facing, 'medium' = team-level, 'low' = individual.

metric.description: A single quantified metric from an achievement. Value MAY be '[NEEDS METRIC: ...]' when the source CV lacks a verifiable number.

value.description: Numeric or descriptive value. Use '[NEEDS METRIC: <what would fit>]' when the source Markdown contains no verifiable number — NEVER invent a value.

dates.description: Date range for an experience or education entry. Normalized to YYYY-MM format. End may be 'Present' for current roles.

start.description: Start date in YYYY-MM format (e.g. '2021-03'). Spanish months normalized: Ene→01, Feb→02, Mar→03, Abr→04, May→05, Jun→06, Jul→07, Ago→08, Sep→09, Oct→10, Nov→11, Dic→12.

end.description: End date in YYYY-MM format, or 'Present' for current roles.

earlierExperience.description: Simplified entry for roles older than the primary experience window. No UUID id or achievements array — these are excluded from algorithmic matching.

education.description: Academic degree or certification program entry.

dates.description: Date range for multi-year programs (optional; prefer 'year' for single-year programs).

name.description: Certification name (e.g. 'AWS Solutions Architect', 'Google Project Management Certificate').

metadata.description: Forward-compatible metadata block for schema versioning, provenance tracking, and extensibility.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of the schema used to generate this JSON instance (e.g. '1.0.0').

generatedBy.description: Identifier for the process or agent that generated this JSON (e.g. 'f3-cv-data-model').

migratedAt.description: ISO 8601 date when the JSON was migrated from source Markdown (e.g. '2026-06-25').

lastOptimizedFor.description: Reference code of the last JD this CV was optimized against (e.g. 'AGIL').

version.description: Instance version string for tracking revisions.
